scope_note: QA Collatz-Fibonacci Spectral Cert [502]. CLAIM (narrow): QA mod-9 exhibits the three structural features that enable the Collatz-Fibonacci spectral result (Reyes Jiménez arXiv:2606.02621). (CF_1) QA Singularity (9,9) is the unique period-1 fixed point under sigma — structural analog of Collatz vertex 4 mod 6 (absorbing fixed point removed to reveal Fibonacci dynamics); (CF_2) all 80 non-Singularity pairs have period in {8, 24} (the Pisano hierarchy for m=9), i.e., they inhabit the Fibonacci-attractor orbits — analog of G'\{4} having spectral radius phi; (CF_3) Pisano integer witness: F(24) equiv 0 (mod 9), F(25) equiv 1 (mod 9), the only k in {1,...,23} with F(k) equiv 0 is k=12 with F(13) equiv 8 ≠ 1, proving pi(9)=24 is minimal. Theorem NT compliance: the golden ratio phi is NOT a QA state; it is a continuous observer projection over the discrete F(m+1) counts (Reyes Jiménez establishes phi as spectral radius of subgraph — an observer measurement, not a causal input). All cert checks are pure integer arithmetic. Companion certs: [128] Lean proof track; [499] full Pisano partition; [500] Cosmos G-arithmetic. Primary sources: Reyes Jiménez A.E. (2025) arXiv:2606.02621; Wall D.D. (1960) doi:10.2307/2309169; Wildberger N.J. (2005) ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8. Derived 2026-06-23.
